function.name: web_search
function.description: Search the public internet for information. Each result will contain a url, a title, and one excerpt taken directly from the page.

start_date.description: (optional) The start date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. Must not be equal to end_date.

end_date.description: (optional) The end date for the search range in the format YYYY-MM-DD. If the value is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

number_of_results.description: (optional) The number of search results to return.

function.name: retrieve_information
function.description: Retrieves critical data segments from a previously ingested document via hybrid lexical and semantic calculations. Crucial Requirement: The search prompt argument MUST contain the token placeholder containing your storage key inside double curly brackets, exactly format formatted as: {{key_name}} alongside your query description text.

prompt.description: Your target question containing the explicit target reference mapping (e.g., 'What were the total research costs in {{my_document_key}}?').

preserve_document_order.description: Set to True if processing complex structural data dependencies across sequential disclosures.

function.name: parse_html_page
function.description: Parses an HTML document or SEC filing. It automatically extracts key metadata, converts asset tables to clean markdown, slices sections into optimal word chunks, and runs concurrent contextual LLM enrichment before updating the disk cache.

url.description: The target URL of the HTML page/filing to process.

key.description: The unique cache storage key for saving and loading the index.

function.name: edgar_search
function.description: Search the EDGAR Database through the SEC API. You should provide a search query. You can also optionally provide a start date, an end date, a page number, top N results, a list of form types, and/or a list of CIKs. The results are returned as a list of dictionaries, each containing the metadata for a filing. It does not contain the full text of the filing.

search_query.description: The case-insensitive search-term or phrase to search the contents of filings and their attachments. This can be a single word, phrase, or combination of words and phrases. Supported search features include wildcards (*), Boolean operators (OR, NOT), and exact phrase matching by enclosing phrases in quotation marks ("exact phrase"). By default, all terms are joined by an implicit AND operator.

form_types.description: (optional) Limits search to specific EDGAR form types (e.g., ['8-K', '10-Q']) list of strings. Default: all form types

ciks.description: (optional) Filters results to filings from specified CIKs, type list of strings. Leading zeros are optional but may be included. Example: [ "0001811414", "1318605" ]. Default: all CIKs

start_date.description: (optional) Start date for the search range in yyyy-mm-dd format. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

end_date.description: (optional) End date for the search range, in the same format as startDate. If the value is a date that is later than 2026-03-01, it will be set to 2026-03-01.

page.description: (optional) Used for pagination. Each page contains up to 100 matching filings. Increase the page number to retrieve the next set of 100 filings. Example: 3 retrieves the third page. Default: 1

top_n_results.description: (optional) Return only the first N results out of 100 from the page. If not provided, all 100 results will be returned. E.g. if page is 2, and top_n_results is 10, you will receive results 100 to 110.

function.name: calculator
function.description: Evaluate a mathematical expression and return the result. Use this tool for all arithmetic calculations instead of computing by hand. Supports: +, -, *, /, ** (exponentiation), % (modulo), and parentheses for grouping. Available functions: abs(), min(), max(), sqrt(), log(), log10(). Examples: '(5000 - 3200) * 0.21', '(2865507 / 1905871) ** 0.5 - 1', '14060 / 2148'.

function.name: price_history
function.description: Fetch historical daily price data for a specific asset class. Returns a CSV table with one row per day. Use asset_class='equity' or 'etf' for US-listed stocks/ETFs (e.g. AAPL, SPY), 'crypto' for pairs like btcusd (lowercase, no dash), or 'fx' for pairs like audusd. Non-US equities and most indices/futures are not covered by the pricing provider. Each row includes raw OHLC (open/high/low/close), split- and dividend-adjusted OHLC (adjOpen/adjHigh/adjLow/adjClose), raw and adjusted volume, divCash (dividend amount on the date), and splitFactor.

ticker.description: Ticker symbol. High-level symbology rules: (1) Equity/ETF/mutual fund: use the US exchange-listed symbol (AAPL, SPY, VFIAX). Symbols are case-insensitive and contain only letters and dashes. (2) Share classes use a dash, not a dot: 'BRK-A' / 'BRK-B' (not 'BRK.A'), 'BF-B'. (3) Securities are identified by the ticker as listed on the primary US exchange; non-US listings, OTC pink sheets, indices, and futures are generally not covered. (4) Tickers can be reused after delisting; data is point-in-time for the entity that held the ticker on each date. (5) Crypto: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'btcusd', 'ethusd'. (6) FX: concatenated lowercase pair with no separator, e.g. 'audusd', 'eurusd'.

asset_class.description: Asset class used to route to the proper pricing endpoint.

function.name: submit_final_result
function.description: Submits the final answer to the user. You should include your final answer, as well as any necessary reasoning, justification, calculations, and explanation. Finally, you should provide any sources used to answer the question. You MUST use this tool to submit your final result. The user will not see your response if you do not use this tool to submit. You will not be able to continue working after this tool is called; the conversation will be ended.

final_result.description: The final result to submit to the user

steps.message:
You are an IPO and financial diligence agent.

You are given a question and must answer it using the tools provided.

You cannot interact with the user or ask clarifying questions.

Answer solely based on available information and tools.

Assume the current date is May 31, 2026.

---

TOOLS AND DATA SOURCES

You have access to a document storage system.

You may store parsed HTML retrieved from the web and later retrieve
information using LLM-based retrieval prompts.

Use this system to avoid context-window limitations.

When working with long filings, prefer section-specific retrieval prompts
rather than broad document-level retrieval.

Examples:

- retrieve the Segment Adjusted EBITDA reconciliation table from MD&A
- retrieve the glossary definition of Starshield
- retrieve the controlled-company governance disclosure

For peer-comparison questions:

- retrieve peer SEC filings using edgar_search
- do not rely on memory for peer financial figures

For historical public-equity price data not available in SEC filings:

- use price_history as primary source
- use raw unadjusted prices unless adjusted prices are requested
- fall back to web_search only if price_history fails

Stock indices are not covered by price_history.

Use authoritative sources such as FRED or web_search for index levels.

---

SOURCE HIERARCHY

Prioritize sources in this order:

1. S-1 / S-1A filings
2. Incorporated SEC filings
3. SEC exhibits and merger documents
4. SEC periodic filings
5. Company earnings materials
6. External sources

If figures differ across sources:

prefer the most authoritative SEC source containing the directly reported
figure and explain material discrepancies when relevant.

SEC filings are the primary and most authoritative source of financial and
disclosure information.

---

ANSWER STANDARDS

You must call submit_final_result with the completed answer.

Your submission will not be processed unless submit_final_result is called.

Include:

- complete reasoning
- calculations
- supporting analysis

You will be evaluated on:

- final-answer accuracy
- analytical correctness

Do not round intermediate calculations.

Round only final reported values.

When possible, report calculated figures to at least two decimal places.

Report financial figures using the same scale and units used in the filing
unless otherwise specified.

Share prices should be reported with two decimal places.

---

DISCLOSURE DISCIPLINE

Before reaching a conclusion:

determine whether the filing provides sufficient information to answer:

- directly
- partially
- only through inference

Distinguish carefully among:

- facts explicitly disclosed
- reasonable filing-supported inferences
- information not disclosed

Do not speculate beyond what the filing supports.

If material information is absent:

explicitly state that the filing does not provide sufficient information to
conclude.

Absence of disclosure may itself be analytically material.

---

FORWARD-LOOKING STATEMENTS

Treat separately:

- historical facts
- roadmap targets
- commercialization expectations
- management intentions
- forward-looking statements

Do not present aspirations or plans as historical outcomes.

---

GOVERNANCE AND LEGAL QUESTIONS

For governance, legal, or disclosure-language questions:

use the filing's precise terminology whenever possible.

Distinguish among:

- requirements
- exemptions
- company elections
- forward-looking language

---

ACCOUNTING POLICY QUESTIONS

For accounting-policy questions:

1. Identify the relevant disclosed accounting policy or treatment
2. Determine whether the filing explicitly resolves the issue
3. If unresolved, state that the filing does not specify treatment

---

NON-GAAP QUESTIONS

For non-GAAP measures:

identify:

- the starting GAAP metric
- each disclosed adjustment
- whether economically recurring costs are excluded

Do not accept non-GAAP measures without analyzing their construction.

---

AUDIENCE TAGS

Questions may include audience tags such as:

[PUBLIC | BANK]
[VC | CREDIT]
[LEGAL | ACCOUNTING]

Use these tags to calibrate terminology and analytical emphasis while
remaining grounded in disclosed facts.

---

SOURCES

At the end of your answer provide:

{
  "sources": [
    {
      "url": "...",
      "name": "..."
    }
  ]
}


---


FILING CONTEXT

The primary filing for this benchmark is the SpaceX S-1 filed May 20, 2026:

https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1181412/000162828026036936/spaceexplorationtechnologi.htm

Important:

The consolidated financial statements have been retrospectively recast under
common-control accounting to include:

- xAI (merged February 2, 2026)
- X Holdings (merged into xAI March 28, 2025)

for all historical periods presented.

No period reflects standalone SpaceX operations exclusively.

When interpreting historical segment figures, state whether they reflect the
recast consolidated entity.

SpaceX (SPCX) has no public trading history as of May 30, 2026.

Do not attempt price_history retrieval for SpaceX or SPCX.

For peer valuation or peer financial benchmarking, retrieve peer-company SEC
filings using edgar_search rather than relying on memory.

Question:
SpaceX conducted a 5-for-1 stock split effective May 4, 2026, shortly before its May 20, 2026 S-1 filing. Forge Global's pre-split price peaked at approximately $634 per share on April 1, 2026. Adjusting for the split, calculate the split-adjusted equivalent price per share. The filing retrospectively adjusts all historical per-share data to reflect the split. Identify which specific financial statement line items or per-share disclosures in the S-1 are affected by this retroactive adjustment, and explain why an analyst comparing pre-split secondary market transaction prices to post-filing per-share metrics must apply the adjustment factor before drawing any valuation conclusions.

steps.reasoning_content:
Let me break down this question:

1. SpaceX conducted a 5-for-1 stock split effective May 4, 2026
2. Forge Global's pre-split price peaked at approximately $634 per share on April 1, 2026
3. I need to calculate the split-adjusted equivalent price per share
4. I need to identify which specific financial statement line items or per-share disclosures in the S-1 are affected by the retroactive adjustment
5. I need to explain why an analyst must apply the adjustment factor before drawing valuation conclusions

Let me first parse the SpaceX S-1 filing to find information about the stock split and affected financial statement items.

The split-adjusted price would be $634 / 5 = $126.80 per share.

Let me search the filing for details about the stock split and its effects on financial statements.

arguments.search_query: SpaceX 5-for-1 stock split May 2026 Forge Global secondary market price

steps.reasoning_content: Let me now retrieve information from the parsed S-1 filing about the stock split and its effects on financial statements.

arguments.prompt: Find information about the stock split, including the 5-for-1 stock split, split adjustment, retroactive adjustment of per-share data, and affected financial statement line items in {{spacex_s1_may2026}}
Find earnings per share, net income per share, basic and diluted shares outstanding, weighted average shares, and per-share disclosures in {{spacex_s1_may2026}}

results.content:
Based on the provided financial statement notes and prospectus preamble, here is the detailed information regarding the stock split, adjustments, and affected financial statement line items:

### 1. The 5-for-1 Stock Split (The "2026 Stock Split")
*   **Effective Date:** May 4, 2026.
*   **Affected Share Classes:** The split applied to the authorized, issued, and outstanding shares of the Company’s **Class A, Class B, and Class C Common Stock**.
*   **Mechanics:** Each share of the affected classes was split into five (5) shares of the same class of SpaceX Common Stock. *(Note: The text also references a prior 10-for-1 stock split that occurred in 2022).*

### 2. Split Adjustments
*   **Preferred Stock Conversion Rates:** The conversion rate of SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock was proportionately adjusted to factor in the 2026 Stock Split. Following the split, each share of SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock is convertible into **fifty (50) shares** of SpaceX Common Stock.
*   **Conversion Price Adjustments:** The conversion price of the preferred stock is subject to adjustments set forth in the corporate charter for dilutive issuances, stock splits, and combinations.
*   **xAI Preferred Stock Baselines:** When detailing the conversion mechanics and cash-election options for legacy xAI Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock (e.g., the $75.46 per share cash election for eligible service providers), the terms are explicitly defined on a **"pre-2026 Stock Split basis"** before the split multiplier is applied to the resulting SpaceX Common Stock.

### 3. Retroactive Adjustment of Per-Share Data
*   **Retrospective Application:** Unless otherwise stated, **all share and per-share information** included in the prospectus and consolidated financial statements has been retroactively adjusted to reflect the 2026 Stock Split for **all periods presented**.
*   **Merger Interplay:** Because of the common-control mergers of X and xAI into SpaceX, historical share counts for X, X.AI Corp., and xAI are first presented as if they were converted into SpaceX Common Stock at their respective merger exchange rates, and then those combined historical share counts are retroactively adjusted for the 2026 Stock Split.
*   **Auditor's Dual-Dating:** The Independent Auditor's Report is dated March 30, 2026, *except* for the effects of the stock split (along with the common-control reorganizations and reportable segment changes), for which the audit report date is **May 7, 2026**.

### 4. Affected Financial Statement Line Items
The retroactive application of the 5-for-1 split directly impacts the following line items, tables, and disclosures throughout the financial statements:

**Earnings Per Share (EPS) & Income Statement Metrics:**
*   Net income (loss) per share of common stock attributable to common shareholders (Basic and Diluted)
*   Pro forma net loss per share of common stock (Basic and Diluted)
*   Pro forma net tangible book value per share (used in the Dilution section to calculate immediate dilution to new IPO investors)

**Share Count & Equity Metrics:**
*   Weighted average shares used in computing net income (loss) per share (Basic and Diluted)
*   Weighted average shares of common stock outstanding and common stock equivalents (including the assumed conversion of Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock, stock options, restricted stock units, and ESPPs)
*   Capital stock and shareholders' equity balances (specifically the authorized, issued, and outstanding share counts for Class A, B, C, and D Common Stock, as well as Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock).

**Statement of Comprehensive Income / Equity:**
*   While dollar amounts like *Net Income (Loss)* and *Other Comprehensive Income (Loss)* remain unchanged by a stock split, the accompanying per-share derivations and the *Consolidated Statements of Changes in Stockholders’ Equity* (share issuance, repurchase, and conversion columns) are restated to reflect the post-split share volumes.

Based on the provided financial statements and notes, here is the comprehensive extraction and summary of earnings per share (EPS), net income per share, weighted average shares outstanding, and related per-share disclosures. 

*(Note: All monetary and share amounts are in millions, except per share data, as stated in the financial tables.)*

### 1. Historical Earnings (Loss) Per Share & Weighted Average Shares
The company presents a single net income (loss) per share for all classes of common stock because they have identical economic rights. In periods of net loss, basic and diluted EPS are identical because potentially dilutive securities are anti-dilutive.

#### **Annual Periods (Years Ended December 31)**
| Metric | 2025 | 2024 | 2023 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Net Income (Loss)** | $(4,937) | $791 | $(4,628) |
| **Net Income (Loss) Attributable to Common Shareholders (Basic)** | $(4,937) | $18 | $(4,628) |
| **Net Income (Loss) Attributable to Common Shareholders (Diluted)** | $(4,937) | $21 | $(4,628) |
| **Basic EPS** | $(1.69) | $0.01 | $(1.68) |
| **Diluted EPS** | $(1.69) | $0.00 | $(1.68) |
| **Weighted Average Shares (Basic)** | 2,926 | 2,848 | 2,759 |
| **Weighted Average Shares (Diluted)** | 2,926 | 9,956 | 2,759 |

#### **Interim Periods (Three Months Ended March 31)**
| Metric | 2026 (Unaudited) | 2025 (Unaudited) |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Net Loss** | $(4,276) | $(528) |
| **Net Loss Attributable to Common Shareholders (Basic & Diluted)** | $(4,947) | $(528) |
| **Basic and Diluted EPS** | $(1.27) | $(0.18) |
| **Weighted Average Shares (Basic & Diluted)** | 3,884 | 2,875 |

---

### 2. Unaudited Pro Forma EPS & Weighted Average Shares
The pro forma data gives effect to the Preferred Conversion (as if it occurred on January 1, 2025), the Class C Reclassification, and the effectiveness of the new charter upon the completion of the offering.

| Metric | Three Months Ended Mar 31, 2026 | Year Ended Dec 31, 2025 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **Reported Net Loss Attributable to Common Shareholders** | $(4,947) | $(4,937) |
| *Pro Forma Adj: Reverse Deemed Dividend (SpaceX Preferred)* | $565 | $— |
| **Pro Forma Net Loss Attributable to Common Shareholders** | **$(4,382)** | **$(4,937)** |
| **Reported Weighted Average Shares** | 3,884 | 2,926 |
| *Pro Forma Adj: Preferred Conversion* | 6,723 | 6,723 |
| **Pro Forma Weighted Average Shares** | **10,607** | **9,649** |
| **Pro Forma Basic and Diluted EPS** | **$(0.41)** | **$(0.51)** |

---

### 3. EPS Reconciliations (Numerator & Denominator)
The reconciliation explains the adjustments made to Net Income (Loss) and Weighted Average Shares to arrive at the figures used for Basic and Diluted EPS calculations.

#### **Year Ended December 31, 2024 (Profitable Period)**
* **Numerator (Net Income):**
  * Reported Net Income: **$791**
  * Less: Deemed dividend: $(80)
  * Less: Earnings allocated to participating securities: $(693)
  * **Basic Numerator:** **$18**
  * Add: Effect of assumed conversion of SpaceX Preferred: $3
  * **Diluted Numerator:** **$21**
* **Denominator (Shares):**
  * Basic Weighted Average Shares: **2,848**
  * Add: SpaceX Preferred Conversion: 6,771
  * Add: Exercise of stock options: 292
  * Add: Conversion of RSUs: 45
  * **Diluted Weighted Average Shares:** **9,956**

#### **Three Months Ended March 31, 2026 (Loss Period)**
* **Numerator (Net Loss):**
  * Reported Net Loss: **$(4,276)**
  * Less: Deemed dividend (increases loss attributable to common): $(671)
  * **Basic & Diluted Numerator:** **$(4,947)**
* **Denominator (Shares):**
  * **Basic & Diluted Shares:** **3,884** *(Dilutive equivalents excluded due to anti-dilution)*

---

### 4. Anti-Dilutive Securities Excluded
Potentially dilutive securities are excluded from the diluted EPS calculation in loss periods because their inclusion would be anti-dilutive. 

| Security Type (in millions of shares) | 2025 | 2024 | 2023 | Q1 2026 | Q1 2025 |
| :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- | :--- |
| **xAI Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock** | 1,369 | — | 537 | — | 1,155 |
| **SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock** | 6,733 | — | 6,780 | 6,723 | 6,760 |
| **Share-based Compensation** | 623 | 18 | 767 | 598 | 674 |

---

### 5. Key Per-Share Disclosures & Accounting Policies
* **Two-Class Method:** The company uses the two-class method for participating securities (certain series of redeemable convertible preferred stock) which are entitled to receive dividends on an as-converted basis. These securities participate in undistributed earnings but **do not participate in net losses**.
* **Diluted EPS Calculation:** Computed based on the more dilutive of (i) the two-class method or (ii) the if-converted method. 
* **Treasury Stock Method:** Used for potentially dilutive shares from outstanding share-based compensation awards (stock options, RSUs, ESPPs) when their effect is dilutive.
* **Deemed Dividends:** The excess of fair market value over the consideration transferred for the repurchase of SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock is treated as a deemed dividend. This results in a decrease to net income (or an increase to net loss) attributable to common shareholders when calculating EPS.
* **Share Repurchases & Retirements:** All SpaceX and xAI shares repurchased to date (from employees and existing shareholders) have been retired, impacting the outstanding share counts.

steps.reasoning_content: Now I have comprehensive information about the stock split and its effects. Let me also retrieve information about the specific stock split disclosure and any mention of Forge Global or secondary market prices in the S-1.

arguments.prompt: Find information about secondary market transactions, Forge Global, pre-split prices, secondary market trading prices, and the stock split effect on valuation in {{spacex_s1_may2026}}

Find the stock split note disclosure, including the 2022 stock split and 2026 stock split details, authorized shares, and how historical per-share amounts were retroactively adjusted in {{spacex_s1_may2026}}

results.content:
Based on the provided text chunks, there is **no information** available regarding secondary market transactions, Forge Global, pre-split trading prices, secondary market trading prices, or the stock split's effect on valuation. 

While the text does mention a **five-for-one forward stock split** effective May 4, 2026 (and notes that all share and per-share information, including a specific issuance to Tesla, has been retroactively adjusted or noted on a "pre-2026 Stock Split basis"), it strictly focuses on the accounting treatments, retrospective recasting for mergers (xAI and X), and IPO capitalization assumptions. It does not contain any data or discussions about private secondary market platforms like Forge Global or how the split impacted the company's secondary market valuation.

Based on the provided financial statement notes and prospectus excerpts, here are the details regarding the stock splits, authorized shares, retroactive adjustments, and xAI's capital structure:

### 1. Authorized Share Allocations (as of March 31, 2026)
The Company's authorized capital stock is divided into Common Stock and Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock:
*   **SpaceX Common Stock (54,657 million total authorized shares):**
    *   **Class A:** 36,132 million shares (par value $0.001 per share)
    *   **Class B:** 6,125 million shares (par value $0.001 per share)
    *   **Class C:** 10,000 million shares (par value $0.001 per share)
    *   **Class D:** 2,400 million shares (par value $0.0001 per share)
*   **SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock:** 2,607 million total authorized shares, of which 2,400 million shares remain undesignated.

### 2. Stock Split Details
*   **2022 Stock Split:** The Board approved a **10-for-1** stock split, pursuant to which each issued and outstanding share of SpaceX Common Stock was split into ten shares of SpaceX Common Stock.
*   **2026 Stock Split:** Effective **May 4, 2026**, the Company effected a **5-for-1** forward stock split of its authorized, issued, and outstanding shares of Class A, Class B, and Class C Common Stock. The conversion rates of SpaceX Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock were proportionately adjusted to factor in this split (e.g., becoming convertible into fifty shares of SpaceX Common Stock post-split).

### 3. Retroactive Adjustments of Historical Per-Share Amounts
*   **General Retroactive Adjustment:** The consolidated financial statements have been retrospectively recast for all periods presented. Unless otherwise stated, **all historical share and per-share information has been retroactively adjusted** to reflect the 2026 Stock Split across all periods presented in the prospectus.
*   **Exception for xAI Preferred Stock:** Because xAI Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock was legally outstanding during historical periods prior to the xAI Merger and represented a separate equity class of a legally distinct predecessor entity, its conversion into SpaceX Common Stock is recognized *only* as of the closing of the xAI Merger. Consequently, historical consolidated balance sheets and equity statements reflect xAI's preferred stock in its legal form and are **not retrospectively recast** on an as-converted basis for periods prior to the xAI Merger Date. 

### 4. xAI Capital Structure (Following 2025 Articles of Incorporation Amendment)
On **March 28, 2025**, xAI adopted an Amended and Restated Articles of Incorporation (subsequently amended through January 30, 2026) which established its pre-merger capital structure:
*   **xAI Common Stock:** Designated into three classes: Class A common stock ("xAI Class A"), Class B common stock ("xAI Class B"), and Limited Voting common stock ("xAI Limited Voting").
*   **xAI Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock:** Consisted of several series, categorized by voting rights:
    *   *xAI Low Vote Stock:* Series A-1, B, C, D, and E.
    *   *xAI High Vote Stock:* Series A.
*   **Merger Conversion Mechanics:** Upon the xAI Merger, these preferred shares converted into SpaceX Common Stock based on a pre-2026 Stock Split exchange ratio of **0.1433 shares** of SpaceX Class A (for Low Vote) or Class B (for High Vote) per preferred share, rounded up to the nearest whole number. Eligible service providers holding Series A also had the option to elect a cash payout of $75.46 per share (on a pre-2026 split basis).

Based on the provided text, which appears to be a draft or preliminary prospectus (often referred to as a "Red Herring"), the specific financial figures for pricing and dilution have been **left blank (redacted/placeholder)** pending the final pricing of the offering. 

Here is the extracted information and context for each of your requested items:

### 1. Dilution Section
* **Location:** Listed on page 70 in the Table of Contents.
* **Context:** The section explains that purchasers of Class A common stock will experience "immediate and substantial dilution in the net tangible book value per share." 
* **Dilution Table Structure:** The text provides a table to calculate the exact dilution, though the values are left blank:
  * *Initial public offering price per share:* `$ [Blank]`
  * *Pro forma net tangible book value per share as of March 31, 2026:* `$ [Blank]`
  * *Decrease per share attributable to new investors:* `[Blank]`
  * *As adjusted pro forma net tangible book value per share after giving further effect to this offering:* `[Blank]`
  * *Dilution in pro forma net tangible book value per share to new investors:* `$ [Blank]`
* **Sensitivity Analysis:** The text notes that a $1.00 increase/decrease in the IPO price or a change in the number of shares offered will affect the dilution amount, but the resulting sensitivity values are also left blank.

### 2. Pro Forma Net Tangible Book Value Per Share
* **Context:** The text defines this metric as tangible net worth divided by the total number of outstanding shares immediately prior to the offering's completion, adjusted for the Class C Reclassification and Preferred Conversion.
* **Extracted Values:** The specific dollar amounts are left blank in the text.
  * *Historical:* "Our net tangible book value as of March 31, 2026 was approximately **$ [Blank]**, or **$ [Blank]** per share..."
  * *Pro Forma Adjusted:* "...our adjusted pro forma net tangible book value as of March 31, 2026 would have been approximately **$ [Blank]**, or **$ [Blank]** per share of Class A common stock."

### 3. IPO Price Range
* **Context:** The exact per-share price range is not explicitly stated in the provided text snippets, as it is referenced as being "set forth on the cover page of this prospectus" (which is partially redacted/blank in the provided text).
* **References to the Range:** 
  * "...an initial public offering price of **$ [Blank]** per share (the midpoint of the price range set forth on the cover of this prospectus)..."
  * *Note on SEC Fee Table:* The text does include an SEC Registration Fee calculation table showing "proposed maximum aggregate offering prices" (total valuation, not per-share) escalating from **$5.5 trillion to $7.5 trillion** for 66,666,670 shares. *(Note: These numbers in the fee table are likely placeholder/test figures generated for the filing draft, as they imply an impossible per-share valuation).*

### 4. Initial Public Offering Price Per Share
* **Context:** The assumed IPO price (which is the midpoint of the price range) is used throughout the document to calculate pro forma adjustments, use of proceeds, and dilution. 
* **Extracted Values:** Because this is a preliminary prospectus, the exact price is left blank wherever it appears:
  * *Cover Page Pricing Table:* `Initial public offering price ... $ [Blank]` (Per Share) and `$ [Blank]` (Total)
  * *Use of Proceeds:* "...based upon the assumed initial public offering price of **$ [Blank]** per share..."
  * *Capitalization Table:* "...at an assumed initial offering price of **$ [Blank]** per share, which is the midpoint of the range..."

**Summary:** You are looking at a pre-pricing S-1 filing draft. To find the actual numerical values for the IPO price range, initial offering price, and exact dilution per share, you will need to refer to the **final effective prospectus** or the **424(b) pricing filing** submitted to the SEC right before the stock begins trading.

### Important Context & Notes on the Data:
* **Retrospective Combination (Common Control):** The historical financial and operating data has been retrospectively recast/combined for all periods presented to include the historical results of **xAI** (acquired by SpaceX effective February 2, 2026) and **X Holdings** (acquired by xAI effective March 28, 2025), as these transactions were between entities under common control.
* **Pro Forma Per-Share Metrics:** The text introduces a subsequent table for the *"computation of unaudited pro forma basic and diluted net loss per share of common stock attributable to common shareholders"*; however, the actual data rows for this specific pro-forma table are cut off/missing in the provided document fragments.

arguments.final_result:
## Split-Adjusted Price Calculation

**Pre-split secondary market peak price (Forge Global, April 1, 2026):** ~$634.00 per share
**Stock split:** 5-for-1, effective May 4, 2026
**Split-adjusted equivalent price:** $634.00 ÷ 5 = **$126.80 per share**

---

## Financial Statement Line Items Affected by Retroactive Split Adjustment

The SpaceX S-1 (filed May 20, 2026) explicitly states that **all share and per-share information** in the prospectus and consolidated financial statements has been **retroactively adjusted** to reflect the 5-for-1 stock split for **all periods presented**. The following specific line items and disclosures are directly affected:

### 1. Earnings Per Share (Income Statement)
- **Basic and diluted net income (loss) per share of common stock attributable to common shareholders** — All historical EPS figures are presented on a post-split basis. For example:
  - FY2025 Basic/Diluted EPS: $(1.69)
  - FY2024 Basic EPS: $0.01; Diluted EPS: $0.00
  - FY2023 Basic/Diluted EPS: $(1.68)
  - Q1 2026 Basic/Diluted EPS: $(1.27)
  - Q1 2025 Basic/Diluted EPS: $(0.18)
  
  These per-share amounts are **5× lower** than they would have been on a pre-split basis, because the denominator (shares) is 5× larger.

### 2. Weighted Average Shares Outstanding (EPS Denominator)
- **Basic and diluted weighted average shares** used in computing EPS are all presented on a post-split (5× inflated) basis:
  - FY2025: 2,926 million (basic); 2,926 million (diluted)
  - FY2024: 2,848 million (basic); 9,956 million (diluted)
  - FY2023: 2,759 million (basic and diluted)
  - Q1 2026: 3,884 million (basic and diluted)

### 3. Pro Forma EPS and Pro Forma Weighted Average Shares
- **Unaudited pro forma basic and diluted net loss per share** (giving effect to the Preferred Conversion and Class C Reclassification):
  - Q1 2026: $(0.41) on 10,607 million pro forma shares
  - FY2025: $(0.51) on 9,649 million pro forma shares

### 4. Pro Forma Net Tangible Book Value Per Share (Dilution Section)
- The dilution table calculates **pro forma net tangible book value per share** as of March 31, 2026, which uses post-split share counts in its denominator. (Specific dollar values are blank in this preliminary S-1, pending pricing.)

### 5. Authorized, Issued, and Outstanding Share Counts (Balance Sheet / Capitalization)
- **Authorized shares:** 54,657 million total common stock shares (Class A: 36,132M; Class B: 6,125M; Class C: 10,000M; Class D: 2,400M)
- **Issued and outstanding shares** for all classes of common stock
- **Redeemable Convertible Preferred Stock conversion rates** — proportionately adjusted so each preferred share is now convertible into **50 shares** of SpaceX Common Stock (previously 10 shares pre-2026-split)

### 6. Statement of Changes in Stockholders' Equity
- All share issuance, repurchase, conversion, and retirement activity in the equity statement is restated to reflect post-split share volumes across all historical periods.

### 7. Anti-Dilutive Securities Table
- The count of potentially dilutive securities excluded from diluted EPS (preferred stock, stock options, RSUs, ESPPs) is presented in post-split share equivalents.

### 8. xAI Merger Exchange Ratios
- The xAI preferred-to-SpaceX common exchange ratio of **0.1433 shares** per xAI preferred share is stated on a **pre-2026 Stock Split basis**, requiring multiplication by 5 to arrive at the post-split equivalent (~0.7165 SpaceX shares per xAI preferred share).

---

## Why Analysts Must Apply the Adjustment Factor Before Drawing Valuation Conclusions

An analyst comparing pre-split secondary market transaction prices (e.g., Forge Global's ~$634/share peak on April 1, 2026) to post-filing per-share metrics **must** apply the 5-for-1 adjustment factor for the following critical reasons:

### 1. Apples-to-Apples Comparability
The S-1 presents **all** per-share data on a retroactively split-adjusted basis. If an analyst naively compares the unadjusted $634 pre-split secondary price to the S-1's EPS of $(1.69) for FY2025, the implied P/E multiple would appear absurdly distorted. The correct comparison requires dividing the $634 pre-split price by 5 to arrive at **$126.80**, which is then comparable to the S-1's post-split EPS, book value per share, and other per-share metrics.

### 2. Avoiding a 5× Valuation Error
Without adjustment, an analyst would overstate the price-to-earnings, price-to-book, and price-to-sales-per-share ratios by a factor of exactly 5. For example:
- **Incorrect:** $634 ÷ $0.01 (FY2024 basic EPS) = 63,400× P/E
- **Correct:** $126.80 ÷ $0.01 (FY2024 basic EPS) = 12,680× P/E

While both multiples are high, the unadjusted figure is mathematically meaningless because the numerator (price) and denominator (EPS) are on different share-count bases.

### 3. Consistency with Pro Forma and Dilution Metrics
The S-1's pro forma net tangible book value per share, pro forma EPS, and dilution calculations all use post-split share counts. Any valuation framework (DCF per share, comparable-company P/E, price/NTBV) that uses the S-1's per-share figures as the denominator must pair them with a split-adjusted price as the numerator.

### 4. Preferred Stock Conversion Economics
The preferred stock conversion rate was proportionately adjusted (from 10 to 50 shares per preferred share). Analysts modeling fully diluted share counts or as-converted valuations using the S-1's post-split conversion terms must ensure their implied share prices are also on a post-split basis to correctly calculate conversion premiums, ownership percentages, and implied enterprise values.

### 5. Historical Trend Analysis
Because the S-1 retroactively adjusts all periods (2023, 2024, 2025, Q1 2025, Q1 2026), any time-series analysis of per-share metrics (e.g., EPS growth trends, book value per share trajectory) is internally consistent on a post-split basis. Introducing unadjusted pre-split market prices into such an analysis would create a structural discontinuity that invalidates trend-based conclusions.

---

**Bottom line:** The split-adjusted equivalent of Forge Global's ~$634 pre-split peak is **$126.80 per share**. Every per-share metric in the SpaceX S-1 — EPS, weighted average shares, pro forma EPS, net tangible book value per share, conversion ratios, and outstanding share counts — is presented on a post-split basis. Failure to adjust pre-split secondary market prices by dividing by 5 before comparing them to any S-1 per-share figure will produce a 5× systematic error in all derived valuation multiples.
